ogTitle: Darwin River Dam vs Delamere: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Darwin River Dam and Delamere.
heading: Darwin River Dam vs Delamere — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Darwin River Dam, Northern Territory and Delamere, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Delamere has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Darwin River Dam (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Darwin River Dam or Delamere safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Darwin River Dam has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Delamere scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Darwin River Dam scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Delamere scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Darwin River Dam or Delamere?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Delamere has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
